creator: anthony.maher@uts.edu.au
text:
Updated to latest version 1.0b3 and actually have it fetch correctly!


# This is a shell archive.  Save it in a file, remove anything before
# this line, and then unpack it by entering "sh file".  Note, it may
# create directories; files and directories will be owned by you and
# have default permissions.
#
# This archive contains:
#
#	py-numpy
#	py-numpy/Makefile
#	py-numpy/files
#	py-numpy/files/patch-system_info.py
#	py-numpy/pkg-descr
#	py-numpy/pkg-plist
#	py-numpy/distinfo
#
echo c - py-numpy
mkdir -p py-numpy > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - py-numpy/Makefile
sed 's/^X//' >py-numpy/Makefile << 'END-of-py-numpy/Makefile'
X# New ports collection makefile for:	numpy
X# Date created:				24 July 2006
X# Whom:					Tony Maher
X#
X# $FreeBSD$
X#
X
XPORTNAME=	numpy
XPORTVERSION=	1.0b3
XCATEGORIES=	math python
XMASTER_SITES=	http://numpy.scipy.org/:doc \
X	${MASTER_SITE_SOURCEFORGE:S/$/:source/}
XMASTER_SITE_SUBDIR=	numpy/:source
XPKGNAMEPREFIX=	${PYTHON_PKGNAMEPREFIX}
XDISTFILES=	numpy-${PORTVERSION}${EXTRACT_SUFX}:source \
X	numpy.pdf:doc
XEXTRACT_ONLY=numpy-${PORTVERSION}${EXTRACT_SUFX}
X
XMAINTAINER=	tonymaher@optusnet.com.au
XCOMMENT=	The New Numeric Extension to Python
X
XLIB_DEPENDS=	lapack.3:${PORTSDIR}/math/lapack \
X	blas.1:${PORTSDIR}/math/blas
X
XUSE_PYTHON=	yes
XUSE_PYDISTUTILS=yes
X
X.include <bsd.port.pre.mk>
X
Xpre-fetch:
X.if ${PYTHON_REL} < 200
X	@${ECHO} "Py-numpy doesn't work with Python versions < 2.0."
X	@${ECHO} "Please use the py-numeric17 port for these."
X	@${FALSE}
X.endif
X
Xpost-extract:
X	@${CP} ${DISTDIR}/numpy.pdf ${WRKDIR}
X
Xpost-install:
X.if !defined(NOPORTDOCS)
X	@${MKDIR} ${DOCSDIR}
X	${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKDIR}/numpy.pdf ${DOCSDIR}
X.endif
X
X.include <bsd.port.post.mk>
END-of-py-numpy/Makefile
echo c - py-numpy/files
mkdir -p py-numpy/files > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - py-numpy/files/patch-system_info.py
sed 's/^X//' >py-numpy/files/patch-system_info.py << 'END-of-py-numpy/files/patch-system_info.py'
X--- numpy/distutils/system_info.py.orig	Thu Jul 27 19:44:41 2006
X+++ numpy/distutils/system_info.py	Thu Jul 27 19:39:18 2006
X@@ -811,7 +811,7 @@
X     dir_env_var = 'ATLAS'
X     _lib_names = ['f77blas','cblas']
X     if sys.platform[:7]=='freebsd':
X-        _lib_atlas = ['atlas_r']
X+        _lib_atlas = ['atlas_r', 'g2c']
X         _lib_lapack = ['alapack_r']
X     else:
X         _lib_atlas = ['atlas']
END-of-py-numpy/files/patch-system_info.py
echo x - py-numpy/pkg-descr
sed 's/^X//' >py-numpy/pkg-descr << 'END-of-py-numpy/pkg-descr'
XFrom the website:
X---------------------------------------------------------------------------
XThe fundamental package needed for scientific computing with Python is
Xcalled NumPy.  This package contains:
X
X    * a powerful N-dimensional array object
X    * sophisticated (broadcasting) functions
X    * basic linear algebra functions
X    * basic Fourier transforms
X    * sophisticated random number capabilities
X    * tools for integrating Fortran code.
X
XNumPy derives from the old Numeric code base and can be used as a
Xreplacement for Numeric.   It also adds the features introduced by numarray
Xand can also be used to replace numarray.  
X
XNote: 	Development for Numeric has ceased, and users should transisition to
X	NumPy as quickly as possible. 
X---------------------------------------------------------------------------
X
XWWW: http://numpy.scipy.org/
END-of-py-numpy/pkg-descr
echo x - py-numpy/pkg-plist
sed 's/^X//' >py-numpy/pkg-plist << 'END-of-py-numpy/pkg-plist'
